pr.title: match some more easy stage getter functions
pr.author: snuffysasa

summary: Matched a small run of stage camera/blast-zone getter functions by moving them from handwritten assembly in asm/melee/gr/stage.s into C implementations in src/melee/gr/stage.c. The functions directly return fields from the global stage_info structure, including cam_info camera parameters and blast_zone limits adjusted by camera offsets.

key_files.role: Removed matched assembly implementations for stage getter functions func_80224AE8 through func_80224B80.
Added C equivalents for the removed stage getter functions using stage_info field accesses.

what_changed: Deleted 71 lines of assembly from asm/melee/gr/stage.s for func_80224AE8, func_80224AF8, func_80224B08, func_80224B18, func_80224B28, func_80224B38, func_80224B50, func_80224B68, and func_80224B80.
Added C implementations of the same nine functions in src/melee/gr/stage.c.
Implemented simple camera-info getters returning stage_info.cam_info.x20, x24, cam_fixed_zoom, cam_track_ratio, and cam_track_smooth.
Implemented blast-zone camera-adjusted getters returning right/left plus cam_x_offset and top/bottom plus cam_y_offset.
No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
smart_moves: Targeted very small leaf functions whose assembly patterns were straightforward lis/addi/lfs/blr getter sequences, making them low-risk candidates for early decompilation.

Used existing struct field names where available, such as cam_zoom_rate, cam_fixed_zoom, cam_track_ratio, cam_track_smooth, blast_zone, cam_x_offset, and cam_y_offset, instead of preserving raw numeric offsets in C.
Preserved the arithmetic behavior of the assembly for blast-zone helpers by adding the camera offset field to the relevant blast-zone edge.
Left unknown camera fields as neutral placeholder names x20 and x24, avoiding unsupported semantic guesses.
decomp_lessons: Simple global-struct getter functions are good incremental matching targets because each assembly load maps cleanly to a struct field access.
When an assembly getter loads two floats and performs fadds, model it directly as a C expression combining the two corresponding struct fields.
Use named struct fields when known, but keep placeholder offset-style field names for fields whose meaning has not been established.
Removing matched assembly and adding equivalent C in the same PR makes the decomp progress easy to audit.
Even small PRs can validate that a contributor's decomp workflow is correct, especially for low-risk functions.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions remained in address-based form: func_80224AE8, func_80224AF8, func_80224B08, func_80224B18, func_80224B28, func_80224B38, func_80224B50, func_80224B68, func_80224B80.
Known stage-global data was accessed through the existing global name stage_info.
Camera substructure fields used cam_info with descriptive names where known: cam_fixed_zoom, cam_track_ratio, cam_track_smooth, cam_x_offset, cam_y_offset.
Unknown cam_info members were named x20 and x24, reflecting their offsets rather than guessed meanings.
Blast-zone fields were expressed as stage_info.blast_zone.right, left, top, and bottom.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched assembly functions consisting of loading the stage_info address with lis/addi, loading a float with lfs, and returning with blr as direct f32 getters.
Mapped lfs offsets 0x20 and 0x24 from stage_info to stage_info.cam_info.x20 and stage_info.cam_info.x24.
Mapped lfs offset 0x2c to stage_info.cam_info.cam_fixed_zoom, 0x28 to cam_track_ratio, and 0x30 to cam_track_smooth.
Mapped functions using fadds to C additions between blast_zone edge fields and camera offset fields.
For horizontal adjusted limits, matched blast_zone.right or left plus cam_info.cam_x_offset.
For vertical adjusted limits, matched blast_zone.top or bottom plus cam_info.cam_y_offset.
review_feedback: The PR body notes the author was new to decompilation and asked whether the insignificant commit and PR process looked correct.
No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were included in the provided PR context, so there is no recorded reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename func_80224AE8 through func_80224B80 to semantic stage or camera getter names.
Search for later struct cleanup around stage_info.cam_info.x20 and stage_info.cam_info.x24 to identify their meanings.
Compare later stage.c formatting or style fixes, since this PR added simple functions with mixed void parameter usage and extra trailing blank lines.
Find other stage.s getter-removal PRs that use the same stage_info field-access matching pattern.
